column4: Document compliance and data-sharing requirements for KYC handoff
column14:
Hierarchy / linkage
- Issue: TGTM-1086 (Task) - Document compliance and data-sharing requirements for KYC handoff
- Parent: TGTM-138 - Build account creation and KYC handoff flow (Resolved in this extract).
- Grandparent / Epic context: TGTM-13 - Build developer and investor conversion funnel mechanics
- Hierarchy path: TGTM-13: Build developer and investor conversion funnel mechanics > TGTM-138: Build account creation and KYC handoff flow > TGTM-1086: Document compliance and data-sharing requirements for KYC handoff

Original intent
Capture any compliance, privacy, consent, and data retention requirements that must be met before sending lead or profile data to an external KYC/AML provider.

How we are doing this
Complete this task as a focused execution unit under the parent Story. Use the parent context to avoid isolated work: every output should improve the relevant page/flow, reduce conversion friction, strengthen trust/compliance, or make lead/account handoff more measurable and operationally usable.

Preliminary solution
Use the approved compliance narrative and create a reviewable content block/checklist that explains limits, eligibility, risks, consent, and no-offer boundaries in plain language; route final wording through legal/compliance before publishing. This supports the broader solution: Implement a conversion-state workflow that captures investor/developer intent, creates or updates the user profile, maps profile fields into the selected KYC/AML provider payload, stores handoff events for auditability, and gives the user clear next-step messaging after submission.

Expected deliverable
A signed-off implementation brief/specification with required fields, owners, workflow decisions, and review notes.

Implementation notes
- Work type: analysis/specification.
- Primary domain: Websites & Conversion for the Blocks Estate GTM campaign.
- Use reusable page sections/components wherever possible so future campaign pages can be launched faster.
- Maintain compliance-safe language, especially around tokenized real estate, eligibility, risks, no-offer boundaries, KYC/AML, fees, and investor/developer claims.
- Ensure each conversion path has clear CTA ownership, source tracking, form behavior, confirmation messaging, and downstream operational routing.
- Document assumptions, open questions, and stakeholder decisions directly in Jira before closing the item.

Dependencies / inputs
KYC/AML provider requirements, consent language, user profile schema, CRM lead fields, privacy/data-retention requirements, compliance review notes, product account-state model.

Measurement / success signals
handoff completion rate, failed handoff rate, KYC start rate, KYC completion rate, time from signup to KYC initiation, support tickets by failure reason.

Acceptance criteria
- The work output directly supports the parent Story and is attached, linked, or documented in Jira.
- Relevant stakeholders can review the output without needing additional explanation.
- Dependencies, assumptions, and open decisions are documented before the issue is moved to Done.
- Compliance-sensitive language is reviewed and does not imply an unapproved offer, guarantee, or unsupported claim.

column4: Build developer and investor conversion funnel mechanics
column14:
Hierarchy / linkage
- Issue: TGTM-13 (Epic) - Build developer and investor conversion funnel mechanics
- Parent: None - this is a top-level Epic in the extract.
- Grandparent / Epic context: None.
- Hierarchy path: TGTM-13 Epic: Build developer and investor conversion funnel mechanics

Original intent
Hierarchy/context:
Epic: TGTM-13 - Build developer and investor conversion funnel mechanics
Child stories in this epic: TGTM-142 - Build funded subscription tracking; TGTM-141 - Build subscription start tracking; TGTM-140 - Build document download tracking; TGTM-137 - Define investor funnel KPI instrumentation; TGTM-136 - Define developer funnel KPI instrumentation.

Business intent:
Build measurable conversion funnels from awareness to consideration, evaluation, conversion, activation, retention, KYC/profile completion, subscription, funding, reporting, and repeat investment.

How we are doing this:
make the GTM funnel measurable from first touch through conversion, funding, activation, retention, and repeat engagement for both developers and investors. The work should be executed as a connected reporting system, not as isolated dashboards. Every dashboard or funnel report should trace back to an approved KPI definition, a source-of-truth mapping, a calculation rule, a refresh owner, and an operating review cadence.

Preliminary solution:
instrument the critical developer and investor funnel events, define attribution/cohort rules, create event pipelines and fact tables, and connect the resulting data to conversion reporting dashboards. Start with a central KPI dictionary, a data-source map, a staging/calculation layer, one dashboard page per major domain, and a weekly KPI review template that converts results into owner-assigned actions.

Expected outputs:
- Approved hierarchy of stories, tasks, and subtasks for analytics/reporting delivery.
- KPI dictionary covering all 90-day and 12-month GTM targets.
- Data-source and attribution map for each measurable funnel or dashboard metric.
- Dashboards with target-vs-actual, trends, quality status, and commentary/action sections.
- Reporting cadence that defines refresh timing, owner accountability, escalation, and decision logging.

Acceptance criteria:
- Each child story has clear metric definitions, data inputs, target ranges, and owner workflow.
- Dashboard outputs reconcile to documented source systems or explicitly flag unresolved data gaps.
- Leadership can use the reporting pack to identify performance, blockers, risks, and next actions without manual explanation.
- Any assumptions, exclusions, and data-quality caveats are visible in the reporting documentation.

How we are doing this
Coordinate the end-to-end build developer and investor conversion funnel mechanics domain as a GTM workstream: define the website/conversion architecture, sequence child Stories, standardize page templates and conversion mechanics, and make sure execution is measurable, compliant, reusable, and connected to downstream sales, CRM, product, and KYC workflows.

Preliminary solution
Establish a modular website and conversion operating model made of audience-specific landing pages, reusable page templates, clear CTA patterns, gated lead capture, trust/compliance content, analytics instrumentation, and handoff workflows that move visitors into waitlist, assessment, partner inquiry, or KYC/account creation paths.

Expected deliverable
A coordinated Epic-level GTM website/conversion workstream with child Stories completed, cross-functional approvals captured, and measurable funnel paths ready for campaign traffic.

Implementation notes
- Work type: implementation/configuration.
- Primary domain: Websites & Conversion for the Blocks Estate GTM campaign.
- Use reusable page sections/components wherever possible so future campaign pages can be launched faster.
- Maintain compliance-safe language, especially around tokenized real estate, eligibility, risks, no-offer boundaries, KYC/AML, fees, and investor/developer claims.
- Ensure each conversion path has clear CTA ownership, source tracking, form behavior, confirmation messaging, and downstream operational routing.
- Document assumptions, open questions, and stakeholder decisions directly in Jira before closing the item.

Dependencies / inputs
Brand and positioning strategy, compliance/legal guidance, product readiness, CRM/lead routing, analytics taxonomy, page design system, website/CMS access, approved source content.

Measurement / success signals
page completion, CTA engagement, form conversion, QA pass rate, stakeholder approval.

Acceptance criteria
- All child Stories have clear scope, owners, dependencies, and acceptance criteria.
- Website/conversion architecture supports reusable pages, compliant messaging, lead capture, analytics, and downstream handoff.
- Stakeholders can trace each page or flow from audience intent through CTA, submission, CRM/KYC routing, and reporting.
